author: Stephanie Nour
body:
It must have taken a lot of strength to write this post. I’ve read a post left by a friend of his on his Facebook wall, and I quote: ” He raised the bar for humanity. If you loved Randy, be more like him, it’s what he would have wanted.”  
I thought that truly captured it…

author: Burgo
body: That’s beautiful Stephanie… and oh-so-true. I’ll be trying to live up to that.

author: Stephanie Nour
body: Oh I didnt write it, but I sure will try to live up to it as well. And through that I’m sure I’ll get to appreciate Randy even more… :)

author: Jesta
body:
A beautiful post. I too struck up an online friendship with Randy and I’m gutted he’s gone. He was inspirational.

“Randy was filled with just so much positivity, you really felt like he was going to change the world.” – I totally agree. It’s such a shame when a light so bright is extinguished. :(

author: Niki Acosta
body: Randy’s sister Crystal was my sister’s BFF growing up, and my heart is heavy with this news. Randy was a lot like my late father — with a knack for the guitar and a positive attitude that was contagious. In fact, during one of Randy’s first trips to Vancouver, he took my father’s guitar with him. Now two brilliant hands, gone too soon, have touched that guitar. When my dad passed in 1994, there was no YouTube, no easy way to distribute music, and internet involved a couple of tones followed by clicks as one dialed their way to communicate. I am grateful that Randy’s presence online is available for the world. His music is timeless. His legacy almost infinite. You will be missed, Randy. Hugs and love to the Ponzio family.
